heading: Abatement of first tier taxes in certain cases
label: 4962 Abatement of first tier taxes in certain cases
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 4962 was renumbered .section 4963 of this title

Amendments2007—Subsec. (b). substituted “D, or G” for “or D”.Pub. L. 110–1721997—Subsec. (b). substituted “subchapter A, C, or D” for “subchapter A or C”.Pub. L. 105–341987—, struck out “private foundation” before “first tier taxes” in section catchline.Pub. L. 100–203, § 10712(b)(4)Subsec. (a). , substituted “any qualified first tier tax” for “any private foundation first tier tax” in closing provisions.Pub. L. 100–203, § 10712(b)(2)Subsec. (b). , added subsec. (b) and struck out former subsec. (b) “Private foundation first tier tax” which read as follows: “For purposes of this section, the term ‘private foundation first tier tax’ means any first tier tax imposed by subchapter A of chapter 42, except that such term shall not include the tax imposed by section 4941(a) (relating to initial tax on self-dealing).”Pub. L. 100–203, § 10712(b)(1)Subsec. (c). , added subsec. (c).Pub. L. 100–203, § 10712(b)(1)

children.text: a taxable event was due to reasonable cause and not to willful neglect, and
such event was corrected within the correction period for such event,

children.heading: Qualified first tier tax
children.label: b Qualified first tier tax
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “qualified first tier tax” means any first tier tax imposed by subchapter A, C, D, or G of this chapter, except that such term shall not include the tax imposed by section 4941(a) (relating to initial tax on self-dealing).

children.heading: Special rule for tax on political expenditures of section 501(c)(3) organizations
children.label: c Special rule for tax on political expenditures of section 501(c)(3) organizations
children.text: In the case of the tax imposed by section 4955(a), subsection (a)(1) shall be applied by substituting “not willful and flagrant” for “due to reasonable cause and not to willful neglect”.
